Actinic Keratosis
 Discussed with patient that these are considered to be precancerous and are primarily caused by long-term exposure to ultraviolet (UV) radiation from the sun but can be caused from other sources. Management options were discussed, including: cryotherapy, topical therapies (5FU, imiquimod, etc.). Risks and benefits of each reviewed, with the primary goal of treatment to prevent the progression of the lesion. Patient educated on the importance of sun protection.
Patient opted to proceed with the following treatment: 
 
- Patient opted to proceed with application of Amzeluo PDT to Left Forearm today. 
- Application to sit for 60 minutes followed by blue light for 16:40 seconds. 
- Application done by MA: AM 
- Post care instructions provided to patient. PAU.

procedure:
Debridement (DBR)
Location: R Leg
Quantity: 1
Method: Debridement
Choice: Partial Thickness 
The patient gave informed consent for this procedure. After consent was obtained, attention was turned toward the area of interest which was disinfected and cleaned with alcohol. Local anesthetic, 2% lidocaine with epinephrine was infiltrated around the area of interest. The superficial layers of the site were removed by Shave method using a Number 15. Pressure was applied to achieve hemostasis. Aquaphor was applied at the wound site and it was bandaged. Patient tolerated the procedure well and wound care instructions were reviewed and given to the patient. 
Post treatment instructions were given to the patient. Patient aware to call the office for any signs of infection which includes erythema, edema, exudate, tenderness in the area treated.
examination: - scaly, erythematous plaque located on right distal lower leg
